title: Large codebase developer gets faster agent searches
story: As an iSDLC user with a large codebase (10K-500K files), I want agent searches to complete in sub-second time so that my analysis and discovery workflows are not slowed down by sequential grep scans.
acceptance_criteria: Searches with modality 'indexed' return results in under 1 second on a 500K file codebase
The speed improvement is transparent -- agents use it automatically when available
grep-glob baseline continues to work when indexed backend is unavailable

title: Developer installs indexed backend during setup
story: As an iSDLC user running 'isdlc init', I want the setup pipeline to detect my project size, recommend an indexed search backend if beneficial, and install it with a single confirmation so that I don't have to research and configure search tools manually.
acceptance_criteria: Detection identifies project scale and Python availability
Recommendation is shown with a clear explanation of the benefit
Installation completes with a single 'yes' confirmation
MCP server is auto-configured in .claude/settings.json
Setup never blocks or fails if I decline or if installation fails

title: Developer never manually maintains the search index
story: As an iSDLC user with the indexed backend installed, I want the index to stay current automatically as I edit code so that I never need to think about rebuilding or refreshing it.
acceptance_criteria: File creates, modifications, and deletions are reflected in the index automatically
The file watcher runs in the background without user intervention
Standard directories (node_modules, .git, vendor) are excluded from indexing

title: Developer on any platform can use indexed search
story: As an iSDLC user on macOS, Linux, or Windows, I want the indexed search backend to work on my platform so that I am not excluded from the performance improvement.
acceptance_criteria: Installation succeeds on macOS (Intel and Apple Silicon)
Installation succeeds on Linux (common distributions)
Installation succeeds on Windows 10/11 with Python 3.8+
File watching uses platform-appropriate mechanisms

title: Small codebase developer is not pressured to install
story: As an iSDLC user with a small codebase (under 10K files), I want the indexed backend to be presented as optional rather than recommended so that I am not pressured to install something I don't need.
acceptance_criteria: Detection marks indexed backend as 'optional' for small projects
No warning or negative messaging if the user skips installation
grep-glob baseline continues to serve all search needs adequately

title: Agent workflows continue when indexed backend fails
story: As an iSDLC user whose indexed backend has crashed or become unresponsive, I want my agent workflows to continue without interruption so that a backend failure does not block my work.
acceptance_criteria: Router falls back to grep-glob when indexed backend is degraded
A single degradation warning is shown per session, not per query
Agent workflows complete successfully regardless of backend status

title: Developer configures index storage location
story: As an iSDLC user with limited disk space or a preferred storage location, I want to configure where the search index is stored so that I can manage disk usage according to my needs.
acceptance_criteria: Default location follows platform conventions
Location is overridable via CODE_INDEX_DIR environment variable
Index is stored outside the project tree by default (not in git)
